label: Legal Name Change — Illinois
description: A recommended order for updating name and gender marker records across government agencies and institutions in Illinois. Each phase builds on the previous one.

phases.label: Foundation
phases.description: These two steps unlock most of what comes after. SSA is the anchor for employment and tax records. An updated birth certificate is required for first-time passport applications and is one of the strongest identity documents.
phases.note: SSA and birth certificate can be done in either order or simultaneously. SSA is typically faster.

phases.label: Identity Documents
phases.description: Government-issued photo ID and travel documents. An updated driver's license or state ID is required by banks, employers, and many other institutions.
phases.note: The driver's license is required before most phase 3 and 4 updates. Passport and passport card can be done simultaneously.

phases.label: Government Records
phases.description: Voter registration, federal student aid, and tax records.
phases.note: The IRS name update happens automatically after SSA — no separate action needed. Verify it on your next tax return.

phases.label: Digital and Mail
phases.note: These can be done at any point — no dependencies. Many take only a few minutes.
